fields.name: CacheSize
fields.doc: CacheSize specifies the storage capacity for the model cache PersistentVolume and PersistentVolumeClaim (e.g., "100Gi", "500Gi", "1Ti").

fields.name: NodeNames
fields.doc: NodeNames is a list of specific node names to enable model caching on. The operator will label these nodes with kserve/localmodel=worker. Mutually exclusive with NodeSelector.

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector is a label selector that identifies nodes for model caching using pre-existing node labels (e.g., nvidia.com/gpu). The operator will label matching nodes with kserve/localmodel=worker. Mutually exclusive with NodeNames.

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.managementState != 'Managed' || has(self.cacheSize)",message="cacheSize is required when managementState is Managed"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.managementState != 'Managed' || (has(self.nodeNames) && size(self.nodeNames) > 0) || (has(self.nodeSelector) && ((has(self.nodeSelector.matchLabels) && size(self.nodeSelector.matchLabels) > 0) || (has(self.nodeSelector.matchExpressions) && size(self.nodeSelector.matchExpressions) > 0)))",message="one non-empty nodeNames or nodeSelector is required when managementState is Managed"

api_types.doc: KueueManagementSpec struct defines the component's management configuration.

fields.name: ManagementState
fields.doc: Set to one of the following values: - "Managed"   : present for backwards compatibility with OLM upgrades, but not supported at runtime. The operator will reject this value. Use "Unmanaged" or "Removed" instead. - "Unmanaged" : the operator will not deploy or manage the component's lifecycle, but may create supporting configuration resources. - "Removed"   : the operator is actively managing the component and will not install it, or if it is installed, the operator will try to remove it

fields.name: AutoCreateQueues
fields.doc: AutoCreateQueues controls whether the operator automatically creates default ClusterQueue and LocalQueue resources in managed namespaces. When false (the default), the operator skips queue creation entirely, allowing administrators to manage queues via GitOps or other external tooling. HardwareProfiles of type "Queue" continue to reference externally-managed LocalQueues without change.

fields.name: DefaultLocalQueueName
fields.doc: Configures the automatically created, in the managed namespaces, local queue name. Only used when autoCreateQueues is true.

fields.name: DefaultClusterQueueName
fields.doc: Configures the automatically created cluster queue name. Only used when autoCreateQueues is true.

api_types.name: TelemetryConfig
api_types.doc: TelemetryConfig defines configuration for telemetry collection. When enabled, deploys TelemetryPolicy for usage metrics (Limitador) and Istio Telemetry for per-subscription latency tracking.

fields.name: Enabled
fields.doc: Enabled controls whether telemetry resources are deployed. When true, creates TelemetryPolicy for usage metrics and Istio Telemetry for per-subscription latency tracking. Default is true (telemetry enabled).

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics contains configuration for optional metric dimensions/labels.

api_types.name: MetricsConfig
api_types.doc: MetricsConfig defines which dimensions (labels) are captured in telemetry metrics. Each dimension can be enabled or disabled to control metric cardinality and storage costs. Note: subscription, cost_center, and tier dimensions are always emitted for billing and access control.

fields.name: CaptureOrganization
fields.doc: CaptureOrganization enables the organization_id label on metrics.

fields.name: CaptureUser
fields.doc: CaptureUser enables the user label on metrics. Disabled by default for privacy/GDPR compliance.

fields.name: CaptureGroup
fields.doc: CaptureGroup enables the group label on metrics for team-based chargeback. Note: This is a high-cardinality dimension and is disabled by default.

fields.name: CaptureModelUsage
fields.doc: CaptureModelUsage enables the model label on metrics.

api_types.name: APIKeysConfig

fields.name: MaxExpirationDays
fields.doc: MaxExpirationDays is the maximum allowed expiration in days for API keys. When set, users cannot create API keys with expiration longer than this value. Examples: 30 (one month), 90 (three months), 365 (one year). If not set, no expiration limit is enforced.

fields.name: ClientID
fields.doc: ClientID is the OAuth2 client ID. Incoming OIDC tokens must have an azp (authorized party) claim matching this value.

fields.name: TTL

api_types.name: FeatureTracker
api_types.doc: FeatureTracker represents a cluster-scoped resource in the Data Science Cluster, specifically designed for monitoring and managing objects created via the internal Features API. This resource serves a crucial role in cross-namespace resource management, acting as an owner reference for various resources. The primary purpose of the FeatureTracker is to enable efficient garbage collection by Kubernetes. This is essential for ensuring that resources are automatically cleaned up and reclaimed when they are no longer required.

api_types.name: Source
api_types.doc: Source describes the type of object that created the related Feature to this FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: FeatureTrackerSpec
api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerSpec defines the desired state of FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: FeatureTrackerStatus
api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerStatus defines the observed state of FeatureTracker.

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase describes the Phase of FeatureTracker reconciliation state. This is used by OLM UI to provide status information to the user.

api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerList contains a list of FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: CertificateSpec
api_types.doc: CertificateSpec represents the specification of the certificate securing communications of an Istio Gateway.

fields.name: SecretName
fields.doc: SecretName specifies the name of the Kubernetes Secret resource that contains a TLS certificate secure HTTP communications for the KNative network.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type specifies if the TLS certificate should be generated automatically, or if the certificate is provided by the user. Allowed values are: * SelfSigned: A certificate is going to be generated using an own private key. * Provided: Pre-existence of the TLS Secret (see SecretName) with a valid certificate is assumed. * OpenshiftDefaultIngress: Default ingress certificate configured for OpenShift

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector specifies the node selector to use for direct node scheduling. Workloads will be scheduled only on nodes that match all the specified labels.

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations specifies the tolerations to apply to workloads for direct node scheduling. These tolerations allow workloads to be scheduled on nodes with matching taints.

api_types.name: SchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: SchedulingSpec allows for specifying either kueue-based scheduling or direct node scheduling. CEL Rule 1: If schedulingType is "Queue", the 'kueue' field (with a non-empty localQueueName) must be set, and the 'node' field must not be set. CEL Rule 2: If schedulingType is "Node", the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set.

fields.name: SchedulingType
fields.doc: SchedulingType is the scheduling method discriminator. Users must set this value to indicate which scheduling method to use. The value of this field should match exactly one configured scheduling method. Valid values are "Queue" and "Node".

fields.name: Kueue
fields.doc: Kueue specifies queue-based scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Queue".

fields.name: Node
fields.doc: node specifies direct node scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Node".

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Queue' ? (has(self.kueue) && has(self.kueue.localQueueName) && !has(self.node)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Queue', the 'kueue.localQueueName' field must be specified and non-empty, and the 'node' field must not be set"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Node' ? (has(self.node) && !has(self.kueue)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Node', the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set"

api_types.name: KueueSchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: KueueSchedulingSpec defines queue-based scheduling configuration.

fields.name: LocalQueueName
fields.doc: LocalQueueName specifies the name of the local queue to use for workload scheduling. When specified, workloads using this hardware profile will be submitted to the specified queue and the queue's configuration will determine the actual node placement and tolerations.

fields.name: PriorityClass
fields.doc: PriorityClass specifies the name of the WorkloadPriorityClass associated with the HardwareProfile.

api_types.name: ServingSpec
api_types.doc: ServingSpec specifies the configuration for the KNative Serving components and their bindings with the Service Mesh.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name specifies the name of the KNativeServing resource that is going to be created to instruct the KNative Operator to deploy KNative serving components. This resource is created in the "knative-serving" namespace.

fields.name: IngressGateway
fields.doc: IngressGateway allows to customize some parameters for the Istio Ingress Gateway that is bound to KNative-Serving.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is a name Service Mesh Control Plane. Defaults to "data-science-smcp".

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace is a namespace where Service Mesh is deployed. Defaults to "istio-system".

fields.name: MetricsCollection
fields.doc: MetricsCollection specifies if metrics from components on the Mesh namespace should be collected. Setting the value to "Istio" will collect metrics from the control plane and any proxies on the Mesh namespace (like gateway pods). Setting to "None" will disable metrics collection.

api_types.name: GatewaySpec
api_types.doc: GatewaySpec represents the configuration of the Ingress Gateways.

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain specifies the host name for intercepting incoming requests. Most likely, you will want to use a wildcard name, like *.example.com. If not set, the domain of the OpenShift Ingress is used. If you choose to generate a certificate, this is the domain used for the certificate request.

fields.name: Certificate
fields.doc: Certificate specifies configuration of the TLS certificate securing communication for the gateway.

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace where it is deployed. If not provided, the default is to use '-auth-provider' suffix on the ApplicationsNamespace of the DSCI.

fields.name: Audiences
fields.doc: Audiences is a list of the identifiers that the resource server presented with the token identifies as. Audience-aware token authenticators will verify that the token was intended for at least one of the audiences in this list. If no audiences are provided, the audience will default to the audience of the Kubernetes apiserver (kubernetes.default.svc).

fields.name: Auth

api_types.name: NetworkPolicyConfig
api_types.doc: NetworkPolicyConfig defines network policy configuration for kube-auth-proxy. When nil or when Ingress is nil, NetworkPolicy ingress rules are enabled by default to restrict access to kube-auth-proxy pods.

fields.name: Ingress
fields.doc: Ingress defines ingress NetworkPolicy rules. When nil, ingress rules are applied by default (allows traffic from Gateway pods and monitoring namespaces). When specified, Enabled must be set to true to apply rules or false to skip NetworkPolicy creation. Set Enabled=false only in development environments or when using alternative network security controls.

api_types.name: IngressPolicyConfig
api_types.doc: IngressPolicyConfig defines ingress NetworkPolicy rules

fields.name: Enabled
fields.doc: Enabled determines whether ingress rules are applied. When true, creates NetworkPolicy allowing traffic only from Gateway pods and monitoring namespaces.

api_types.name: OIDCConfig
api_types.doc: OIDCConfig defines OIDC provider configuration

fields.name: IssuerURL
fields.doc: OIDC issuer URL

fields.name: ClientID
fields.doc: OIDC client ID

fields.name: ClientSecretRef
fields.doc: Reference to secret containing client secret

fields.name: SecretNamespace
fields.doc: Namespace where the client secret is located If not specified, defaults to openshift-ingress

api_types.name: CookieConfig
api_types.doc: CookieConfig defines cookie settings for OAuth2 proxy

fields.name: Expire
fields.doc: Expire duration for OAuth2 proxy session cookie (e.g., "24h", "8h") This controls how long the session cookie is valid before requiring re-authentication.

fields.name: Refresh
fields.doc: Refresh duration for OAuth2 proxy to refresh access tokens (e.g., "2h", "1h", "30m") This must be LESS than the OIDC provider's Access Token Lifespan to avoid token expiration. For example, if Keycloak Access Token Lifespan is 1 hour, set this to "30m" or "45m".

api_types.name: GatewayConfigStatus
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigStatus defines the observed state of GatewayConfig

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain is the computed gateway domain (subdomain + cluster domain or default) This is the single source of truth for the gateway domain used by all components

api_types.name: GatewayConfig
api_types.doc: GatewayConfig is the Schema for the gatewayconfigs API

api_types.name: GatewayConfigList
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigList contains a list of GatewayConfig

api_types.name: GatewayConfigSpec
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigSpec defines the desired state of GatewayConfig

fields.name: IngressMode
fields.doc: IngressMode specifies how the Gateway is exposed externally. "OcpRoute" uses ClusterIP with standard OpenShift Routes (default for new deployments). "LoadBalancer" uses a LoadBalancer service type (requires cloud or MetalLB).

fields.name: OIDC
fields.doc: OIDC configuration (used when cluster is in OIDC authentication mode)

fields.name: Certificate
fields.doc: Certificate specifies configuration of the TLS certificate securing communication for the gateway.

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain specifies the host name for intercepting incoming requests. Most likely, you will want to use a wildcard name, like *.example.com. If not set, the domain of the OpenShift Ingress is used. If you choose to generate a certificate, this is the domain used for the certificate request. Example: *.example.com, example.com, apps.example.com

fields.name: Subdomain
fields.doc: Subdomain configuration for the GatewayConfig Example: my-gateway, custom-gateway

fields.name: Cookie
fields.doc: Cookie configuration (applies to both OIDC and OpenShift OAuth)

fields.name: AuthTimeout
fields.doc: AuthTimeout is the duration Envoy waits for auth proxy responses. Requests timeout with 403 if exceeded. Deprecated: Use AuthProxyTimeout instead.

fields.name: AuthProxyTimeout
fields.doc: AuthProxyTimeout defines the timeout for external authorization service calls (e.g., "5s", "10s") This controls how long Envoy waits for a response from the authentication proxy before timing out 403 response.

fields.name: NetworkPolicy
fields.doc: NetworkPolicy configuration for kube-auth-proxy

fields.name: ProviderCASecretName
fields.doc: ProviderCASecretName is the name of the secret containing the CA certificate for the authentication provider Used when the OAuth/OIDC provider uses a self-signed or custom CA certificate. Secret must exist in the openshift-ingress namespace and contain a 'ca.crt' key with the PEM-encoded CA certificate.

fields.name: VerifyProviderCertificate
fields.doc: VerifyProviderCertificate controls TLS certificate verification for the authentication provider. When true (default), certificates are verified against the system trust store and providerCASecretName. When false, certificate verification is disabled (development/testing only). WARNING: Setting this to false disables security and should only be used in non-production environments. For production use with self-signed certificates, use ProviderCASecretName instead.

fields.name: EnableK8sTokenValidation
fields.doc: EnableK8sTokenValidation enables Kubernetes service account token validation via TokenReview API. When enabled, kube-auth-proxy validates bearer tokens as service account tokens alongside OAuth/OIDC authentication. This allows service accounts to authenticate via bearer tokens while human users authenticate via OAuth/OIDC.

api_types.name: TracesStorage
api_types.doc: TracesStorage defines the storage configuration for tracing

fields.name: Backend
fields.doc: Backend defines the storage backend type. Valid values are "pv", "s3", and "gcs".

operator_config.doc:
adminGroups maps each supported platform to its default admin group name.
These groups are assigned administrative privileges in the Auth custom resource.

Platform mappings:
  - SelfManagedRhoai: "rhods-admins" - for self-managed Red Hat OpenShift AI
  - ManagedRhoai: "dedicated-admins" - for managed/hosted Red Hat OpenShift AI
  - OpenDataHub: "odh-admins" - for Open Data Hub deployments

The admin group members have full administrative access to the platform,
including the ability to manage users, configure components, and access
all platform resources.

summary: rhods-operator is a Kubernetes component. deploying 22 workload(s) (aws-cloud-manager-operator, aws-cloud-manager-operator, aws-cloud-manager-operator, azure-cloud-manager-operator, azure-cloud-manager-operator, azure-cloud-manager-operator, coreweave-cloud-manager-operator, coreweave-cloud-manager-operator, coreweave-cloud-manager-operator, controller-manager, controller-manager, controller-manager, controller-manager, controller-manager, controller-manager, controller-manager, rhods-operator, rhods-operator, rhods-operator, rhods-operator, rhods-operator, rhods-operator) with 22 container(s). watching 45 resource type(s). exposing 3 service(s). protected by 1 network policy/policies. connecting to external services: s3. defining 57 API spec type(s) across 227 struct(s) with 596 total fields. with 363 operator config constants (4 image references). exposing 5 Prometheus metrics. reporting 41 status condition types.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Enforces singleton semantics for DSCInitialization v2 resources by denying creation when one already exists, and prevents deletion of DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster object still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that validates and injects 'connection' secrets. When a Notebook is created or updated with the opendatahub.io/connections annotation (comma-separated namespace/name secret references), it verifies each referenced Secret exists, checks the requesting user has 'get' permission on those Secrets via SubjectAccessReview, then mutates the notebook's first container by adding or removing the secrets as envFrom secretRef entries.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v1 resources as singletons: on create, denies if another DSCInitialization instance already exists; on delete, denies if any DataScienceCluster object still exists, preventing removal of the initialization resource while the cluster is active.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: MonitoringServiceName component enabled OR MonitoringServiceName component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutates PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources on create/update by injecting the monitoring.opendatahub.io/scrape=true label when the resource's namespace is labeled for ODH monitoring and the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists, enabling Prometheus to discover these monitors automatically.

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, node selectors, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR into the workload's pod spec. It also handles profile switching (clearing old settings before applying new ones) and profile removal (cleaning up previously injected settings when the annotation is removed).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's built-in default when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and the NIM state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 resources on create and update. On create, enforces a singleton constraint by listing existing DataScienceCluster instances and denying if one already exists, and rejects any DSC where Kueue's managementState is set to Managed. On update, only the Kueue managementState check is applied. Deletions and other operations are always allowed.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's built-in default when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and NIM's state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 resources on create and update. On create, enforces singleton (only one DSC instance allowed) and rejects Kueue with ManagementState=Managed. On update, rejects Kueue Managed state and blocks v1 API modifications when v2-only components are currently Managed, preventing silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion where v2-only fields would revert to defaults.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage URIs, image pull secrets, service accounts, S3 storage keys) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, and cleans up or replaces those injected fields when the annotation is updated or removed.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources (serving.kserve.io) that automatically injects model connection details based on a platform connection annotation. On create/update, it validates the referenced connection Secret, determines the protocol type (URI/HuggingFace, S3, or OCI), and patches the resource accordingly—setting spec.model.uri, injecting imagePullSecrets for OCI, or binding a ServiceAccount for S3 credentials.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook for DSCInitialization, converting between the v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub) API versions by mapping spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields (Phase, Conditions, RelatedObjects, ErrorMessage, Release) bidirectionally so that clients using either API version can interact with the same stored resource.
